question.text: Who qualifies as a "new cadre distributor" for selling mutual funds?

answer.text: Retired government officials with at least 10 years of service
Any person above the age of 60
Any graduate with a finance degree

explanation.text: **The Logic:** New cadre distributors include postal agents, retired government/semi-government officials (Class III and above), retired bank officers (Scale I and above), and other similar persons — all with a minimum of 10 years of service. They get a special certification (Series V-B) with a limited product range. **The Trap:** Simply being above 60 or having a finance degree does not qualify someone as a new cadre distributor. The requirement is specific: retired from government/bank/postal service AND 10+ years of service. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under distributor categories. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Your years of service in government or banking give you credibility with clients. Use your experience and trustworthiness as your biggest asset when building your MF distribution practice.
